mission.title: fluentd: Configuration as yaml/json
mission.description: Configuration as yaml/json. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current fluentd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your fluentd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
helm list -n fluentd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working fluentd installation.

steps.title: Review fluentd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluentd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get configmap -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluentd
```
I've been trying to setup a FluentD puppet module that is able to build configuration from resource calls, however, the configuration syntax makes this very difficult.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Configuration as yaml/json
steps.description:
From this spec, puppet-fluentd supports `<server>` sections: https://github.com/mmz-srf/puppet-fluentd/blob/develop/spec/defines/fluentd_match_spec.rb#L33
If `secondary` doesn't work, then adding `secondary` support to puppet-fluentd is good for other users.

Hm... supporting yaml instead of DSL
```yaml
fluentd::match:
    pattern: 'nginx.*'
    priority: 10
    options:
      type: copy
      store:
       - type: 'file'
         path: '/var/log/fluent/myapp'
         time_slice_format: '%Y%m%d'
         time_slice_wait: '10m'
         time_format: '%Y%m%dT%H%M%S%z'
         compress: 'gzip'
       - type: 'mongo'
         host: 'fluentd'
         port: '27017'
         database: 'fluentd'
         collection: 'test'
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get events -n fluentd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Configuration as yaml/json" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
From this spec, puppet-fluentd supports `<server>` sections: https://github.com/mmz-srf/puppet-fluentd/blob/develop/spec/defines/fluentd_match_spec.rb#L33
If `secondary` doesn't work, then adding `secondary` support to puppet-fluentd is good for other users.

Hm... supporting yaml instead of DSL may be a good idea for this problem.
codeSnippets:
fluentd::match:
    pattern: 'nginx.*'
    priority: 10
    options:
      type: copy
      store:
       - type: 'file'
         path: '/var/log/fluent/myapp'
         time_slice_format: '%Y%m%d'
         time_slice_wait: '10m'
         time_format: '%Y%m%dT%H%M%S%z'
         compress: 'gzip'
       - type: 'mongo'
         host: 'fluentd'
         port: '27017'
         database: 'fluentd'
         collection: 'test'

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluentd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
